summary:
Claim: β-Oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids produces propionyl-CoA as a final product.  
Evidence: - Odd-chain fatty acids yield one residual two-carbon fragment post-complete β-oxidation, which forms propionyl-CoA rather than acetyl-CoA. - Propionyl-CoA enters the methylmalonic acid pathway and is converted to succinyl-CoA for entry into the TCA cycle.  
Answer: B

Answer: B. Answer choice: B
